response:
<> To replace one URL with another in PHP you can use str_replace which handles multiple occurrences easily. Here is the code snippet that does exactly what you need without using any dots in this explanation. $text = 'New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

'; $oldUrl = 'http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1'; $newUrl = 'http://yahoo.com/abc1'; $updatedText = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $text); echo $updatedText; This will output the string with both the href attribute and the visible link text updated. Remember that str_replace replaces all instances by default. Now for your palindrome request here is one: racecar.
